highlight.title: Major Schools White Paper Coming Early 2026 to Reform SEND System
highlight.summary: <cite index="3-1,3-4">A long-awaited Schools White Paper is expected in early 2026, setting out reforms to the Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) system with focus on earlier intervention, improved local provision and clearer accountability</cite>. <cite index="6-16,6-17">The DfE's landmark schools White Paper in early 2026 will set early expectations on behaviour and attendance, building on existing commitments and providing schools with clearer direction</cite>.

resources.title: BBC Bitesize KS3 Science — Refreshed Content for Y7-Y9
resources.why_now: For Y7-Y9 students: <cite index="13-2,13-3">BBC Bitesize is refreshing its KS3 core subjects, launching 100+ new guides all linked to the KS3 curriculum and designed to be interactive, engaging and to support students' progress through their KS3 journey</cite>. Perfect for current KS3 students needing structured science revision.

resources.title: Oak National Academy — Free KS3-GCSE Resources with AI Support
resources.why_now: For teachers and students: <cite index="21-1,21-4,21-5">Schools report 'absolutely fabulous' results using Oak's new science curriculum for KS3 and KS4 with improved engagement and sequencing of smaller ideas building up to bigger concepts</cite>. <cite index="22-4">Teachers can now use AI-powered lesson assistant Aila to help plan and develop KS3 lessons</cite>.

resources.title: Secondary School Transfer Applications for September 2026
resources.why_now: For parents with Y6 children: <cite index="41-1">March 2nd 2026 is National Allocation Day when parents throughout England will learn which secondary school place has been offered for September 2026</cite>. <cite index="45-14,45-15">Online admissions opened 1 September 2025 with application deadline of 31 October 2025</cite> — results coming very soon for families.

resources.title: Evidence-Based GCSE Revision Strategies for 2026 Exams
resources.why_now: For GCSE students and parents: <cite index="32-12">Research identifies practice testing and distributed practice as the top two study strategies to boost learning</cite>. <cite index="31-2,31-30,31-31">Students should start creating revision timetables at least 12-16 weeks before first exam as this provides the framework for all other techniques</cite> — crucial timing for May 2026 GCSEs.

claude_take: <cite index="3-10,3-11">2026 is poised to be a pivotal year for UK education, marked by significant policy reform alongside ongoing financial and workforce pressures with increased accountability and a continued focus on consistency across the system</cite>. The combination of major SEND reforms, new curriculum requirements, and academy alignment changes means families need reliable, evidence-based resources more than ever to navigate this changing landscape successfully.
